Adjunct points: Shixuan or Jing-well points bloodletting, ST44 (Neiting, clear Stomach heat), points by involved joint, SP10 (Xuehai)

Point rationale: Involved joint Ashi bloodletting drains the joint heat-toxin; GV14 (Dazhui), LI11 (Quchi) clear heat; SP9 (Yinlingquan) drains damp (for damp-heat); Jing-well points bloodletting drains heat; together they clear heat, drain damp, and free the collaterals.

External therapy & care: Elevate the limb, rest, drink plenty of water; avoid pungent-spicy warm food (for damp-heat avoid high-purine); acute gout etc. coordinate with Internal Medicine (see gout chapter).

Classical formula: Baihu Jia Guizhi Tang (★Classical Original★) / Xuan Bi Tang

Heat-exuberance: Baihu Jia Guizhi Tang (Shi Gao, Zhi Mu, Gui Zhi, Gan Cao, Jing Mi); damp-heat Bi: Xuan Bi Tang (Fang Ji, Xing Ren, Hua Shi, Lian Qiao, Zhi Zi, Yi Yi Ren, Ban Xia, Can Sha, Chi Xiao Dou)

Baihu Jia Guizhi Tang clears heat and frees Bi, for heat-exuberant red swelling; Xuan Bi Tang clears heat, drains damp, and frees Bi, for damp-heat Bi, matching heat-predominant (or damp-heat) exuberance.

Textbook reference: Baihu Jia Guizhi Tang / Si Miao San with additions

Heat-Bi: Baihu Jia Guizhi Tang; damp-heat Bi: Si Miao San (Cang Zhu, Huang Bai, Yi Yi Ren, Niu Xi) add Ren Dong Teng, Luo Shi Teng; gout add Tu Fu Ling, Bi Xie

Baihu Jia Guizhi Tang clears Qi-level heat and frees Bi; Si Miao San clears lower-burner damp-heat, add vine medicinals to free the collaterals. Specific like acute gout see gout chapter (Tu Fu Ling specific herb).

Formula analysis: Baihu Jia Guizhi Tang favors clearing Qi-level heat, for heat-exuberance; Xuan Bi Tang / Si Miao San favor clearing-draining damp-heat, for damp-heat Bi. Choose by heat versus damp-heat. Specific diseases like acute gout see the corresponding chapter (with Internal Medicine coordination, Tu Fu Ling specific herb).

Modifications:

  • Blazing heat-toxin — add Jin Yin Hua, Lian Qiao, Ren Dong Teng, Pu Gong Ying
  • Heavy damp-heat — add Yi Yi Ren, Bi Xie, Tu Fu Ling (especially heavy for gout)
  • Specific joint — navigate to the corresponding chapter (acute gout see gout heat-Bi)

Key point: Heat-Bi (heat predominant) clears heat and frees the collaterals, for damp-heat also drains damp; with fever exclude septic arthritis first; acute gout etc. navigate to the corresponding chapter (with Internal Medicine coordination, Tu Fu Ling specific herb).

Recalcitrant/Crippling-Bi (Phlegm-Stasis into Bone · Joint Deformity) / Deficiency-Bi (Qi-Blood Liver-Kidney Deficiency · Malnourishment)

Principle: Recalcitrant/crippling-Bi transforms phlegm, disperses binding, quickens Blood, searches wind; deficiency-Bi supplements Qi-Blood, boosts Liver-Kidney, nourishes the sinew-bone; often addressing both deficiency and excess, sustained effort; navigate to the corresponding chapter

Target muscle / point: Ashi points around the deformed joint (recalcitrant-Bi) / malnourished joint local and corresponding back-shu (deficiency-Bi)

Technique: For recalcitrant-Bi take the deformed joint Ashi points to free the collaterals and disperse binding, encircle locally; for deficiency-Bi take the joint local with Liver-Kidney-Spleen back-shu to supplement deficiency. Chronic treatment requires sustained effort and progression. See each chapter for specific joints.

Safety: Do not forcibly manipulate a deformed joint; deficiency-Bi avoids over-stimulation; tophus ulceration infection no needling (see gout chapter); progressive deformity with wasting excludes tumor and inflammatory disease.

Entry: Insert subcutaneously proximal to the deformed / malnourished joint

Sweep & reperfusion: Sweeping with reperfusion: patient slowly moves the affected joint (pain-free range); recalcitrant-Bi releases chronic stasis, deficiency-Bi frees the collaterals to aid nourishment. See each chapter.

Primary points: Involved joint Ashi points, BL18 (Ganshu), BL23 (Shenshu), BL20 (Pishu), ST36 (Zusanli)

Adjunct points: Recalcitrant-Bi add SP10 (Xuehai), ST40 (Fenglong) to transform phlegm-stasis; deficiency-Bi add CV4 (Guanyuan), SP6 (Sanyinjiao) to supplement; points by involved joint

Point rationale: Recalcitrant-Bi Ashi encircling + SP10 (Xuehai), ST40 (Fenglong) transform phlegm-stasis and disperse binding; deficiency-Bi BL18 (Ganshu), BL23 (Shenshu), BL20 (Pishu), ST36 (Zusanli), CV4 (Guanyuan) supplement Liver-Kidney-Spleen, boost Qi-Blood and nourish the sinew-bone. Addressing both deficiency and excess.

External therapy & care: Recalcitrant-Bi protect the deformed joint, prevent worsening deformity; deficiency-Bi moderate exercise to strengthen the sinew, nutritional supplementation; chronic treatment requires sustained effort. See each chapter.

Classical formula: Guizhi Shaoyao Zhimu Tang (★Classical Original★, crippling-Bi) / Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang (deficiency-Bi)

Crippling-Bi deformity: Guizhi Shaoyao Zhimu Tang with insect medicinals (Quan Xie, Wu Gong, Di Long) to search wind, pick collaterals, transform phlegm-stasis; deficiency-Bi: Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang to supplement Liver-Kidney and boost Qi-Blood

Guizhi Shaoyao Zhimu Tang warmly frees and clears stagnant heat, for crippling-Bi with enlarged deformed joints, add insect medicinals to search wind, pick collaterals, and disperse the phlegm-stasis into bone; Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang supplements Liver-Kidney, boosts Qi-Blood, and nourishes the sinew-bone, for deficiency-Bi malnourishment.

Textbook reference: Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang / crippling-Bi formula (insect searching-picking)

Deficiency-Bi: Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang (pill form, long-term); crippling-Bi: Taohong Yin / Shang Zhong Xia Tong Yong Tong Feng Fang with insect medicinals, Bai Jie Zi, Dan Nan Xing to transform phlegm and disperse binding

Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang supplements deficiency and nourishes the sinew, a key formula for deficiency-Bi; crippling/recalcitrant-Bi transforms phlegm, disperses binding, quickens Blood, and searches wind. Specific like tophi see gout recalcitrant-Bi, rheumatoid see rheumatology specialist.

Formula analysis: Crippling/recalcitrant-Bi (deformity into bone) transforms phlegm, disperses binding, quickens Blood, searches wind (insect medicinals); deficiency-Bi (malnourishment) supplements Qi-Blood, boosts Liver-Kidney, nourishes the sinew-bone (Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang). Often deficiency-excess complex, supporting the upright and dispelling the pathogen together, sustained effort. Navigate specific joints to the corresponding chapter (tophi see gout, Liver-Kidney deficiency see low back Kidney deficiency).

Modifications:

  • Recalcitrant-Bi deformity — add Quan Xie, Wu Gong, Di Long, Bai Jie Zi, Dan Nan Xing
  • Deficiency-Bi Liver-Kidney deficiency — Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang pill long-term, add Lu Jiao Jiao, Gui Ban
  • Specific joint — navigate to the corresponding chapter (tophi/rheumatoid/degeneration each to its chapter)

Key point: Recalcitrant/crippling-Bi transforms phlegm, disperses binding, quickens Blood, searches wind (insect medicinals); deficiency-Bi supplements Qi-Blood, boosts Liver-Kidney, nourishes the sinew-bone (Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang); addressing both deficiency and excess, sustained effort; navigate specific joints to the corresponding chapter; progressive deformity with wasting excludes tumor and inflammatory disease.

⚠ Warning Signs — Module-Wide Safety Exit Framework

Principle: No pattern identification, mainly no needling; screen the six categories of warning signs — exclude/refer/emergency — return to the standard pathway

Target muscle / point: Not applicable

Technique: For any joint Bi pain, first screen the six categories of warning signs; once one stands, suspend needling and complete exclusion, referral, or emergency care.

Safety: Infection with red hot swollen fever no needling; cauda equina syndrome (saddle numbness, bladder-bowel dysfunction) emergency care; progressive nerve damage specialist.

Entry: Not applicable

Sweep & reperfusion: Not applicable.

Primary points: Not applicable

Adjunct points: Not applicable

Point rationale: Before warning signs are cleared, needling may delay recognition of infection, inflammatory arthritis, tumor, fracture, nerve emergency, or visceral emergency.

External therapy & care: The module-wide six safety exits: ① infection (red hot swollen fever) — blood count and inflammatory markers, joint fluid, no needling; ② inflammatory arthritis (symmetric polyarticular morning stiffness >1 hour) — rheumatologic evaluation; ③ tumor (progressive wasting, worsening night pain, pathologic fracture) — imaging specialist; ④ fracture trauma (severe pain and deformity after trauma, easily-missed scaphoid fracture) — imaging; ⑤ nerve emergency (cauda equina syndrome with bladder-bowel dysfunction, progressive strength decline) — emergency or specialist; ⑥ visceral emergency referral (cardiac chest pain radiation, acute abdomen, GI bleeding) — emergency care.

Classical formula: Not applicable

None

The six categories of emergency/serious disease must be excluded and handled first; both prescription and needling delay.

Textbook reference: Not applicable

None

As above.

Formula analysis: This page is the safety-floor framework of the entire pain module: for any joint Bi pain, before acting, first screen the six categories — infection, inflammatory, tumor, fracture, nerve emergency, visceral emergency; only after exclusion differentiate the Bi pathogen and route to the corresponding joint chapter. The warning signs of every chapter derive from this framework.

Key point: The module-wide safety floor: for any Bi pain, first screen the six categories of warning signs (infection / inflammatory / tumor / fracture / nerve emergency / visceral emergency), and only after exclusion differentiate and treat the Bi, routing to the corresponding joint chapter; the warning signs of every chapter belong to this framework.
